name: Sinaloa (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms
the Pacific Ocean and details of the arms
the sun and details of the arms
agriculture, vegetation and details of the arms
the Sierra Madre and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Sinaloa centred.
history: Sinaloa has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.
The arms reference the indigenous and Spanish colonial origins of the state, the Pacific coastline, the Sierra Madre Occidental, and the agricultural and fishing traditions of the region.
